sections.paragraph:
προγράφω 1 aor. προέγραψα. Pass.: 2 aor. προεγράφην; pf. 3 sg. προγέγραπται (Just., D. 43, 3), ptc. προγεγραμμένος (Aristoph., Thu. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, Joseph., Just.).

① to write in advance or before, write before(hand)

ⓐ in the same document in which the word is found (so oft. pap) καθὼς προέγραψα as I have written above Eph 3:3 (Ins of Sinuri no. 46, 17 LRobert ’45 καθότι προγέγραπται; Just., A I, 63, 2 ὡς προεγράψαμεν). τὰ προγεγραμμένα what I have written above (Ins of Sinuri no. 46, 5; PPetr III, 61, 17 [III b.c.]; BGU 1107, 30; 1131, 55 al.) Hv 4, 3, 6; Hs 5, 3, 7. ὁ προγεγραμμένος, τὸ προγεγραμμένον the person or thing mentioned above (POxf 8, 13 [104/5 a.d.] ὁ πρ. μου ἀνήρ; 10, 10; 16) τὰ προγεγραμμένα πρόσωπα the persons mentioned above IMg 6:1 (cp. also Da 3:3). τὸ πρ. (ἀντίγραφον) the forementioned copy MPol 22:3.

ⓑ What is written before, is found in an older document (by another author, as well; cp. Ps.-Clem., Hom. p. 12, 31 Lag.) ὅσα προεγράφη what was written in earlier times (in the γραφή) Ro 15:4.—εἴς τι mark out, designate for someth. (Appian, Bell. Civ. 4, 1 §2 τ. ἐχθροὺς ἐς θάνατον πρ.) of false teachers: οἱ πάλαι προγεγραμμένοι εἰς τοῦτο τὸ κρίμα who for a long time have been marked out (or written about) for this judgment (described in what follows) Jd 4.

② to set forth for public notice, show forth/portray publicly, proclaim or placard in public (γράφω=‘draw, paint’ Hdt., Aristoph. et al.; here and there in Pla.; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 252; PGM 2, 47; 36, 265.—PGM 2, 60 προγράφω of a figure ‘drawn above’) οἷς κατʼ ὀφθαλμοὺς Ἰ. Χρ. προεγράφη ἐσταυρωμένος before whose eyes Jesus Christ was portrayed on a cross Gal 3:1 (many would prefer to transl. placard publicly, set forth in a public proclamation so that all may read: Aristoph., Demosth. et al.; Plut., Demetr. 912 [46, 10], Mor. 408d; Just., A I, 15, 2; IG X, 4, 24; PFlor 99, 11).—M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect
  2. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

ptc. ptc. = participle

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

POxf POxf = Some Oxford Papyri—List 4

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

transl. transl. = translate, translation

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 867
